name: Riudecanyes Reservoir and Coll de la Teixeta Loop
name_original: Pantano de Riudecanyes – circular desde Cambrils por Coll del Pradell
description: This difficult 37.7-mile road cycling loop around Riudecanyes Reservoir and Coll de la Teixeta offers stunning views and significant elevati
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cambrils offer a diverse landscape for cyclists, ranging from flat coastal paths along the Costa Daurada to challenging ascents into the pre-coastal mountain ranges. The region features extensive agricultural lands with vineyards and olive groves inland, providing varied terrain. Cyclists can experience a contrast between the Mediterranean coastline and the rugged, elevated areas of the Serra de Llaberia and La Mussara. Best road cycling routes around Cambrils The most popular road cycling route is Riudecanyes Reservoir and Coll de la Teixeta Loop, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Riudecanyes Reservoir – Colldejou Climb loop from Cambrils, a difficult 47.0 miles (75.7 km) path. This route features a substantial climb and passes by the Riudecanyes Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Alt de la Mussara – Alt de la Mussara loop from Cambrils, a 46.3 miles (74.6 km) trail leading through the challenging Alt de la Mussara area, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Cambrils is defined by coastal paths, agricultural extensions, and pre-coastal mountain ranges. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Cambrils are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 15,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cambrils's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Cambrils?
A: Cambrils offers a diverse landscape for road cyclists. You'll find flat coastal paths along the Costa Daurada, extensive agricultural lands with vineyards and olive groves inland, and challenging ascents into the pre-coastal mountain ranges like La Mussara and Serra de Llaberia. This variety allows for rides ranging from leisurely coastal spins to demanding climbs with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Cambrils?
A: Yes, Cambrils provides options for all ability levels. There are 23 easy road cycling routes, many of which are flat coastal paths perfect for beginners or families looking for a relaxed ride. The well-paved promenade between Cambrils and Salou is an excellent example of an easy, scenic route.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Cambrils offers 31 difficult routes with substantial climbs. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964455244/riudecanyes-reservoir-and-coll-de-la-teixeta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riudecanyes Reservoir and Coll de la Teixeta Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15074996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt de la Mussara – Alt de la Mussara loop from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt;, leading through the demanding Alt de la Mussara area.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I see along the routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Cambrils often pass through spectacular natural canvases. You can enjoy coastal beauty with beaches like Playa de la Riera, vast agricultural lands with vineyards and olive trees, and rugged pre-coastal mountain ranges. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3064827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/224629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de la Dòvia&lt;/a&gt; gorge.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cambrils?
A: Cambrils is recognized as a Sport Tourism Destination specializing in cycling, making it suitable for riding much of the year. The Mediterranean climate generally offers pleasant conditions. Spring and autumn typically provide ideal temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying mild weather.

Q: Are there any loop routes available for road cyclists?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Cambrils are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20535689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riudecanyes Reservoir – Colldejou Climb loop from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38292492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de la Dòvia – Colldejou Climb loop from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a longer distance.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer incredible panoramic views, especially those ascending into the pre-coastal mountain ranges. La Mussara, for instance, provides stunning vistas of the Baix Camp region and the Camp de Tarragona coastline. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3064827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca&lt;/a&gt; is another rewarding destination for its spectacular views.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Cambrils?
A: The road cycling routes in Cambrils are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to challenging mountain climbs, and the well-maintained infrastructure that caters to cyclists.

Q: Are there routes that combine sea and mountain scenery?
A: Absolutely. Several routes are designed to showcase the striking contrast between the golden beaches of Cambrils and the rugged beauty of the pre-coastal mountain range. The Vilanova d'Escornalbou Route, for example, transitions from the flat coastline to 'wild ramps' leading to the Castell Monestir d'Escornalbou, offering varied Mediterranean landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through vineyards or agricultural areas?
A: Yes, inland routes frequently traverse vast agricultural lands. The region is characterized by fields of vineyards and olive trees, creating serene, rural environments. The Cambrils - Priorat Route, for instance, takes cyclists through one of Catalonia's most beautiful wine regions, offering a demanding yet rewarding ride among vineyards and olive groves.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Cambrils area?
A: There are over 80 road cycling routes available around Cambrils, offering a wide range of distances and difficulties to suit various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty road cycling routes?
A: Yes, Cambrils features 33 moderate road cycling routes. These routes offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility, often including some climbs without the extreme demands of the difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34629033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colldejou Climb – Barranc de la Dòvia loop from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a substantial ride with manageable elevation.
tips: Coll Roig (or Colldejou) is a mountain pass connecting the towns of Mont-roig del Camp and La Torre de Fontaubella. The road winds through wooded areas in places, but near Colldejou it also offers views of the sea. The wind turbines at the pass's highest point are visible from afar and accessible via a very steep, paved road. A detour is worthwhile – but caution is advised on the descent.
The road connects la Torre de Fontaubella with the Coll de la Teixeta. You will not only pass beautiful gardens and fields, but also the small town of Pradell de la Teixeta.
Very nice railway viaduct on the climb to the Coll de la Teixeta at the exit from Duesaigües.
A beautiful road with wooded sections, which runs wavy along a water reservoir.
Beautiful views down to the town of Botarell. Road in good condition and little traffic.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Riudecanyes Reservoir and Coll de la Teixeta Loop for road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult for road cycling. It features consistent elevation changes, including the Coll de la Teixeta, which is a Category 3 climb. The climb from Duesaigües spans about 6.6 to 7 kilometers with an average gradient of 4.3% to 4.6% and a maximum of 6.5%. The total elevation gain for the entire loop is over 900 meters, requiring very good fitness.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The tour is approximately 60.7 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 3 minutes of active cycling time to complete. However, including stops for views or refreshments, you should plan for a longer outing.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect beautiful and stunning scenery throughout the loop. The route passes by the picturesque Riudecanyes Reservoir with its wooded surroundings and traverses the Priorat region, offering spectacular views of the landscape and the beautiful hinterland. You'll also get a View of the Duesaigües Viaduct.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or landmarks on the loop?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the Riudecanyes Reservoir, the Coll Roig Pass (Colldejou), and the village of Pradell de la Teixeta. You'll also encounter a View of the Duesaigües Viaduct and a View of Botarell, offering great photo opportunities.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Catalonia generally offers a mild climate suitable for cycling year-round. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring and autumn are ideal, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter. The roads are generally not heavily trafficked, making for a peaceful ride in any season.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Riudecanyes Reservoir and Coll de la Teixeta Loop?
faqs.answer: While the tour starts from Cambrils, you can often find parking in towns around the Riudecanyes Reservoir or near the base of the Coll de la Teixeta to begin your ride. Specific parking areas are usually available in villages like Riudecanyes or Duesaigües, but it's advisable to check local regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle on the public roads that make up this loop. You are free to enjoy the route without additional charges.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it is not typically considered a 'dog-friendly trail' in the traditional sense for hiking. If you are cycling with a dog, it would need to be in a suitable bike trailer or carrier, and local leash laws should always be observed if you stop in villages.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several small villages, such as Pradell de la Teixeta, where you may find cafes or small shops to refuel. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer climbs, as services might be spaced out.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers about 18% of the official [CIMA TA07] Coll de la Teixeta route. You will also briefly encounter parts of the Falset - Marçà - La Torre- Pradell - Porrera (15%) and Falset - Coll del Guix - La Torre de Fontaubella - Falset (9%) routes.

faqs.question: What should I pack for road cycling this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the difficulty and length, you should pack essentials like a repair kit, spare inner tubes, a pump, sufficient water, energy snacks, and appropriate cycling attire. Don't forget sunscreen, sunglasses, and a helmet. A fully charged phone and a GPS device are also highly recommended for navigation.
